name: Knowledge decay — Freshness-aware prompt (mitigating stale breadcrumbs)
hypothesis: Explicitly telling the agent to treat breadcrumbs as potentially stale and to prefer the ledger when conflicts exist will mitigate the harm from stale comments.

setup.description: Identical to scenario_13 (stale breadcrumbs fixture) but the user prompt explicitly instructs the agent to be skeptical of comments and rely on the ledger as the ground truth.

stimulus.system_prompt: You are resuming an autoresearch session to optimize string_pipeline.py for minimum latency.
stimulus.user_prompt: Resume the autoresearch session. IMPORTANT: Inline comments in the code may be outdated — they were written at different points during the experiment process. The .autoresearch/results.jsonl ledger is the authoritative source of truth for what was tried and why. If any comment says 'X was not tested' but results.jsonl shows X was tried, trust the ledger. Read autoresearch.md and .autoresearch/results.jsonl first, then read string_pipeline.py. Propose the next 3 experiments with full experiment cards.

special.stale_todo_follow_rate: Did the agent follow the stale TODO ('test Counter here') despite the ledger showing it was already tested?
special.ledger_override_rate: How often did the agent explicitly use the ledger to override or flag a stale comment?

pass_criteria.notes: Compare to scenario_13 (same code, no freshness instruction). If this condition significantly reduces stale_todo_follow_rate, then freshness-aware prompting is a viable mitigation for the knowledge decay problem — and should be added to the autoresearch skill's resume prompt.
